feedback.text: In my view, it would be useful to take into account other aspects considered unfair by digital users. If I buy a product for expensive money, I also expect to be able to use it. However, some video games seem to be different from distributors. Selling games with the intention of making them unusable after a few years, or even months in certain cases, should not be simply tolerated. If you buy a washing machine, you don’t want the operator to decide otherwise after 2 years and simply stop buying the new version 2.0. Digital fairness should include at least one plan for users to continue to use the product after the closure of online servers, for example, it would be possible for you to approve private servers.
